Great video, Pat! The AMD concept is a classic for a reason. I was curious about the actual expectancy of this setup, so I replicated your logic on Kepler Scout to stress-test it against BTC throughout the entire month of January 2026.

I’ve summarized the backtest metrics below (adjusted to the 15M timeframe for better data consistency):

📊 BACKTEST RESULTS (JAN 2026 - BTC/USDT)
.................................................................................
• Timeframe: 15M
• Total Trades: 164
• Net PNL: +20.00 R
• Win Rate: 28.05%
• Profit Factor: 1.17
• Avg Win / Avg Loss: 3.00 / 1.00
• Expectancy: 0.12 R per trade
.................................................................................

Key Observations:
• Adaptation: Using the 15M timeframe helped filter out some of the intraday noise while keeping the core AMD logic intact.
• Mathematical Edge: The strategy proves that a win rate below 30% is perfectly viable when supported by a solid 1:3 Risk/Reward ratio.
• Performance: It performed exceptionally well during the first week of January, catching the initial rally before the volatility shifted.
• Consistency: Even with 118 losses, the 46 wins were enough to end the month with a strong +20R profit.

It’s a great example of how having a deterministic strategy helps you navigate the noise. Thanks for the inspiration!
